facts.text: South Park was created by Trey Parker and Matt Stone, who remain the show's central creative voices.
South Park premiered on Comedy Central on August 13, 1997.
The series is set in the fictional mountain town of South Park, Colorado.
The four central boys are Stan Marsh, Kyle Broflovski, Eric Cartman, and Kenny McCormick.
South Park grew out of the short film The Spirit of Christmas, which circulated widely as an early internet viral sensation.
The original pilot was animated using actual paper cutouts, and the digital show kept that handmade look as part of its style.
Trey Parker and Matt Stone famously produce many South Park episodes in about six days, allowing the show to react quickly to current events.
The catchphrase 'Oh my God, they killed Kenny!' became one of the most famous recurring jokes in TV animation.
Kenny died in many early episodes, but the show eventually turned the gag into a meta joke about its own history.
Eric Cartman is often considered one of television's most outrageous and memorable comedy characters.
The show's theme song was originally performed by Primus, the eccentric rock band led by bassist Les Claypool.
Isaac Hayes voiced Chef, one of the earliest and most beloved supporting characters on South Park.
South Park: Bigger, Longer & Uncut was released in 1999 and turned the TV series into a hit theatrical movie.
The South Park movie earned an Academy Award nomination for Best Original Song with 'Blame Canada.'
'Chef's Chocolate Salty Balls,' a song tied to the series, reached number one on the UK singles chart.
South Park won Emmy Awards for standout episodes including 'Make Love, Not Warcraft' and 'Margaritaville.'
The series is known for satire that targets politics, celebrities, religion, internet culture, and news cycles from every angle.
The 2005 episode 'Trapped in the Closet' satirized Scientology and became one of the show's most controversial installments.
Butters Stotch gradually evolved from a small side character into one of the series' most beloved breakout favorites.
South Park often breaks from one-off stories into serialized stretches, especially in its later seasons.
The show popularized the phrase 'They took our jobs!' through one of its recurring satirical chants.
The episode 'Scott Tenorman Must Die' is often cited by fans as a turning point that revealed how dark Cartman could become.
South Park has repeatedly mocked its own network, advertisers, and fanbase, which became part of its identity.
The creators later made Team America: World Police and The Book of Mormon, extending their reputation far beyond South Park.
The South Park episode about the restaurant Casa Bonita became so famous that Trey Parker and Matt Stone later bought the real restaurant and helped restore it.
The Canadian characters on South Park are deliberately animated with simple flapping heads as part of an absurd visual joke.
South Park Studios became an official online home for streaming episodes, helping the series thrive in the internet era.
Several South Park episodes were pulled from streaming or syndication because of sensitive depictions of religious figures and threats surrounding them.
The series has been praised for how quickly it can satirize current events while they are still unfolding in real time.
Cartman's songs, rants, and schemes have produced some of the show's most quotable and controversial moments.
The series' crude visual style hides extremely precise writing, editing, and comedic timing.
South Park has spoofed World of Warcraft, anime, superhero movies, Facebook, Yelp, cable companies, and streaming wars.
The video game South Park: The Stick of Truth was co-written by Parker and Stone and was praised for feeling like a playable episode.
South Park: The Fractured but Whole shifted the games toward superhero parody, matching the show's love of pop-culture satire.
Comedy Central signed one of the richest deals in animation history to keep South Park and its specials going well into the 2020s.
Paramount-era South Park specials included Post COVID, The Streaming Wars, Joining the Panderverse, and End of Obesity.
Randy Marsh evolved over time from Stan's dad into one of the series' biggest comic engines, especially in later seasons.
Towelie, the talking towel who constantly tells people not to forget a towel, became an unexpected cult favorite.
South Park's satire is often described as equal-opportunity because it mocks nearly every ideology and cultural trend it touches.
Despite its crude reputation, South Park has become one of the longest-running and most influential cable comedies ever made.
The Spirit of Christmas short film helped launch South Park before the series officially began.
Its early viral spread on the internet played a major role in the show's rise.
The Spirit of Christmas is one of the signature titles or subjects people mention when discussing South Park.
Any overview of South Park usually makes room for The Spirit of Christmas.
The Spirit of Christmas helped define the tone and reputation of South Park.
